name: Pasta and Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons chopped fresh basil, and/or fennel weed
recipeInstructions:
Italian pasta e fagioli is a hearty combination of pasta and bean. Try other dried beans and pasta shapes. This version is meatless; about 300 calories, 12 grams fat (MasterCook III).
1. Carefully pick over the beans to remove any shriveled beans and foreign matter. Rinse, cover with cold water, and soak overnight. Drain and set aside.
2. In a soup pot, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the onion and celery and sauté until soft, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic, sage, and rosemary and saute for an additional minute. Add the bay leaves, drained beans, and enough water to cover by about 1 inch. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
3. In a skillet, heat the remaining 3 tablespoons olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the pancetta or ham, carrot, and fennel (if using) and saute until the pancetta is cooked, about 5 minutes. Add the meat to the beans. Stir in the tomatoes, parsley, and broth and season to taste with salt and pepper. Cover and simmer until the beans are very tender, about 1 hour longer; add more liquid if necessary to keep the beans covered during cooking.
4. About 20 minutes before serving, increase the heat and bring the soup to a boil. Stir in the macaroni and cook until al dente. Just before serving, stir in the minced basil or fennel greens.
5. Ladle the soup into preheated bowls, sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, garnish with basil or fennel sprigs. Pass additional cheese at the table. Alternatively, pour the soup into a container and refrigerate, uncovered until cool, then tightly cover and store for up to 3 days. Slowly reheat by adding herbs, cheese, and garnish.
